my_thoughts: The rejection/unity works: psychological = also esoteric (cannot separate), value = from higher minds (not ordinary), psychological side = more esoteric (has value only because comes from higher minds). The student attempted conceptual separation (seeking to bypass mysticism), which shows they understand: psychological = separate from esotericism (separation). They'll likely accept the rejection/unity. The episode is complete: causal link → rejection → utilitarian → mechanical objection → observation → normalization → separation → rejection/unity. Pattern complete.
accumulated_insight: Four-turn pattern: T1: Student proposed causal link (assuming crisis forces seeking)—rejection: no causal link, adjusts expectations. T2: Student asserted positive consequence (clinging to utilitarian hope)—mechanical objection: fear of losing money/identification, no connection. T3: Student reported observation (confused by lack of appeal)—normalization: esotericism = very difficult, interest = exception. T4: Student attempted conceptual separation ('I am only interested in the psychological side of the system, but not in esotericism')—seeking to bypass mysticism via declares preference and attempts conceptual separation. They understand: psychological = separate from esotericism (separation). The ontological_shift mechanism rejects: psychological = also esoteric (cannot separate), value = from higher minds (not ordinary), psychological side = more esoteric (has value only because comes from higher minds) ('But the psychological side is also esoteric. The system gives you nothing that is not esoteric, for the whole of it comes from minds that are not ordinary minds; it comes from people who understood things. Ordinary systems cannot bring about any change. The psychological side of the system is even more esoteric than the other side, or it would have no value. It has value only because it comes from higher minds'). Pattern complete: causal link proposal → rejection → utilitarian assertion → mechanical objection → observation question → normalization → separation attempt → rejection/unity. They've shifted: psychological = also esoteric (cannot separate), value = from higher minds (rejection/unity).
visible_response:
But the psychological side is also esoteric. The system gives you nothing that is not
esoteric, for the whole of it comes from minds that are not ordinary minds;
it comes
from people who understood things. Ordinary systems cannot bring about any change.
The psychological side of the system is even more esoteric than the other side, or it
would have no value.
It has value only because it comes from higher minds, from their
understanding and their ideas. This must be understood.
We got this system somehow.
If we suppose it has been created by men on the same level as we are, or that it is a
theory, it has no value at all.
